pull.title: mining: pr 33966 followups (disentangle miner startup defaults)
pull.body:
This implement the suggested followups from #33966. Each commit links  to the original comment.

The most important change is the extra asserts added in `miner: ensure block_max_weight is flattened before limit checks`.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Code Coverage & Benchmarks For details see: https://corecheck.dev/bitcoin/bitcoin/pulls/35403. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | ACK | [enirox001](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35403#pullrequestreview-4457437278), [sedited](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35403#pullrequestreview-4548142367), [w0xlt](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35403#pullrequestreview-4550547895) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#35581](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35581) (node: add block template manager and track waitNext fee inflow by ismaelsadeeq) * [#35580](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/35580) (bugfix: compare non-adjusted chunk weight against block weight limit by ismaelsadeeq) * [#33922](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/33922) (mining: add getMemoryLoad() and track template non-mempool memory footprint by Sjors) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first. ### LLM Linter (✨ experimental) Possible places where comparison-specific test macros should replace generic comparisons: - [test/functional/interface_ipc_mining.py] `assert template7 is None` -> `assert_equal(template7, None)` 2026-05-29 07:52:25
Pushed another commit to fix a missing MiniWallet rescan during test cleanup, noticed while working on #33922.

The Alpine CI failure log is 278 MB, is that sane? https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/actions/runs/26592252276/job/78354332966?pr=35403

It hit `feature_dbcrash.py`, so that's probably #35379. (I asked about the log size there)

CI ran on the new warp drive (since #35378).

events.body:
tACK b847626562

Changes look good to me; the miner and functional tests also pass locally. Thanks for following up

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -577,9 +576,11 @@ async def async_routine():
 
     def run_test(self):
         self.miniwallet = MiniWallet(self.nodes[0])
+        # Amount of time the test is allowed to wait or be idle before it should fail.
+        self.default_ipc_timeout = 1000.0 * self.options.timeout_factor
comments.body: Why are you removing the comment that says the type is milliseconds?

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -577,9 +576,11 @@ async def async_routine():
 
     def run_test(self):
         self.miniwallet = MiniWallet(self.nodes[0])
+        # Amount of time the test is allowed to wait or be idle before it should fail.
+        self.default_ipc_timeout = 1000.0 * self.options.timeout_factor
comments.body:
Added unit to the new variable.

It would be nice if Python had something like `std::chrono` so we wouldn't need comments to substitute a type system :-)

> It would be nice if Python had something like `std::chrono` so we wouldn't need comments to substitute a type system :-)

Welcome to Python :)


llm nit: Looks like the latest push itched the llm: 

[test/functional/interface_ipc_mining.py] assert template7 is None -> assert_equal(template7, None)

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -577,9 +576,11 @@ async def async_routine():
 
     def run_test(self):
         self.miniwallet = MiniWallet(self.nodes[0])
+        # Amount of time the test is allowed to wait or be idle before it should fail.
+        self.default_ipc_timeout = 1000.0 * self.options.timeout_factor
comments.body: It keeps bringing that up, but it's not a line we're touching. I'm also not sure if it's actually better.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -577,9 +576,11 @@ async def async_routine():
 
     def run_test(self):
         self.miniwallet = MiniWallet(self.nodes[0])
+        # Amount of time the test is allowed to wait or be idle before it should fail.
+        self.default_ipc_timeout = 1000.0 * self.options.timeout_factor
comments.body: The benefit is only visible on a failure: Instead of just knowing that `template7` is not None, the value of template7 will also be printed. If that isn't better, then `assert_equal` should just be removed from the utils.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -577,9 +576,11 @@ async def async_routine():
 
     def run_test(self):
         self.miniwallet = MiniWallet(self.nodes[0])
+        # Amount of time the test is allowed to wait or be idle before it should fail.
+        self.default_ipc_timeout = 1000.0 * self.options.timeout_factor
comments.body: `assert_equal` is useful when you care about the actual result. That's often the case, but not here. Anything other than `None` is a bug.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -577,9 +576,11 @@ async def async_routine():
 
     def run_test(self):
         self.miniwallet = MiniWallet(self.nodes[0])
+        # Amount of time the test is allowed to wait or be idle before it should fail.
+        self.default_ipc_timeout = 1000.0 * self.options.timeout_factor
comments.body:
> `assert_equal` is useful when you care about the actual result. That's often the case, but not here.

Why not? In other mining contexts we also compare that the result is `None`, because if it wasn't None and the test fails intermittently, it is useful to know why exactly it failed (was it a duplicate block, or an intermittently invalid one, or a bug in the test code itself [return False vs return None], etc ...)

E.g.:

```py
test/functional/p2p_segwit.py:        assert_equal(None, self.nodes[0].submitblock(block.serialize().hex()))
```

Generally I think, if a pattern makes sense, it should be used consistently.

comments.diff_hunk:
@@ -197,7 +198,7 @@ void MinerTestingSetup::TestPackageSelection(const CScript& scriptPubKey, const
     const auto block_package_feerates = BlockAssembler{
         m_node.chainman->ActiveChainstate(),
         &tx_mempool,
-        m_node.mining_args,
+        MergeMiningOptions(options, m_node.mining_args),
comments.body: I like this approach. Using `MergeMiningOptions(options, m_node.mining_args)` ensures we keep the test-specific configurations while still respecting the node-level mining arguments. This is much better than having to choose one or the other
